title: Diplazium huttonii
taxonomy: Diplazium huttonii belongs to the family Athyriaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or lady ferns. The genus Diplazium itself is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Diplazium huttonii is further classified as a species within this genus, being a native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. The classification of Diplazium huttonii is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, and Family: Athyriaceae. This species is also sometimes referred to by its synonym, Diplazium huttonii is sometimes known as Diplazium huttonii.

This plant is rich in health benefits, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.
